title: Crosslinker modified filament and fabric for placement of proppant anti-settling agents in hydraulic fractures
abstract: Compositions for suspending proppants in a hydraulic fracture of a subterranean formation involve a carrier fluid, a plurality of proppants, and a plurality of filaments, which optionally are comprised into a fabric that includes a plurality of connected filaments. The filaments contain crosslinkable sites, e.g. organo- borate, organo-zirconate, organo-titanate, and/or hydroxyl groups. A method of using the compositions includes hydraulically fracturing the subterranean formation to form fractures in the formation, during and/or after hydraulically fracturing the subterranean formation introducing proppants into the fractures, during and/or after hydraulically fracturing the subterranean formation introducing a plurality of filaments into the fractures, where the filaments contact and inhibit or prevent the proppant from settling by gravity within the fractures; and closing the fractures against the proppants. Inhibiting and/or preventing the proppants from settling is improved by the crosslinkable sites crosslinking a polymer present in a fluid contacting the filaments; e.g. a polysaccharide.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1 . A method of suspending proppants (58) in a hydraulic fracture (50) of a subterranean formation (16), the method comprising: hydraulically fracturing the subterranean formation (16) to form fractures (50) in the formation (16); during and/or after hydraulically fracturing the subterranean formation (16), introducing proppants (58) into the fractures (50); characterized by during and/or after hydraulically fracturing the subterranean formation, introducing a plurality of filaments (60) into the fractures (50), where at least a portion of the filaments (60) comprise a plurality of crosslinkable sites (62) on the filaments (60), where the crosslinkable sites (62) are selected from the group consisting of organo-borate, organo-zirconate, organo-titanate, hydroxyl groups, and combinations thereof; and closing the fractures (50) against the proppants (58). 2. The method of claim 1 where the crosslinkable sites (62) on the filaments (60) are chemically bonded to the filaments (60). 3. The method of claim 1 where the fracture (50) comprises at least two faces (52, 54) and the method further comprises the filaments (60) interacting with the faces (52, 54) to inhibit the proppants (58) from settling. 4. The method of claim 3 where introducing the filaments (60) into the fractures (50) comprises a carrier fluid (38) where a proportion of filaments (60) in the carrier fluid (38) interconnect multiple individual filaments (60) into larger connected lengths or a plurality of variable shapes, and which range in concentration from 0.01 pptg to 20 pptg (0.001 to 2.4 kg/m 3 ). 5. The method of claim 1 where the method further comprises: contacting the filaments (60) with a fluid (38) comprising a polysaccharide (64); and crosslinking the polysaccharide (64) with the crosslinkable sites (62) present on the filaments (60). 6. The method of any one of claims 1 where the filament (60) comprises a polymer chain, and where the number of crosslinkable sites (62) along the polymer chain may range from 1 :2 to 1 : 100 molar ratio of crosslinkable sites (62) to polymer chain. 7. The method of any one of claims 1 -6 where the plurality of filaments (60) is selected from the group consisting of polyvinyl alcohols (PVOH), polylactic acids (PLA), polyglycolic acid (PGA), modified polyethylterephthalate (PET), polyesters, polyamides, polycarbonates, cotton, wool, silk, fiberglass, polyurethanes, aramids, acrylics, nylons, polyethylenes, polypropylenes, cellulose, polylactide, modified polyethylene terephthalates, rayons, and combinations thereof. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 -6 where fabrics (30) are present in the fractures (50) and the fabrics (30) comprise a plurality of filaments (32) connected by a structure selected from the group consisting of: woven, non-woven, knitted, laminated, plied, spun, knotted, stacked, and combinations thereof; 

 where at least a portion of the filaments (32) comprise a plurality of crosslinkable sites (62) on the filaments (32), where the crosslinkable sites (62) are selected from the group consisting of organo-borate, organo-zirconate, organo-titanate, hydroxyl groups, and combinations thereof. 9. The method of claim 8 where the fabrics (30) comprise at least a one first filament (32) and at least one second filament (32), and the method further comprises changing a parameter selected from the group consisting of temperature, chemical composition, dissolving at least a portion of one of the filaments (32), dissolving a filament (32), hydrolyzing a filament (32), an actuation due to an applied stress or force or magnetic or electric field, and a combination thereof which parameter changing causes a shape change in at least one of the filaments (32). 10. The method of claim 8 where the fabrics (30) comprise at least one shape-changing filament (32) where the shape-changing filament (32) has a first shape and a subsequent shape and the method further comprises: introducing the fabrics (30) into the fractures (50) when the shape- changing filament (32) has a first shape; and the shape-changing filaments (32) changing shape after a period of time within the fractures (50). 1 1 . The method of any one of claims 1 -6 where the filaments (60) comprise: an average length of from 0.1 inch to 20 inches (0.25 to 51 cm); and an average thickness of from 0.002 inch to 0.2 inch (0.05 mm to 5 mm). 12. The method of any one of claims 1 -6 where the filaments (60) have a minimum aspect ratio of length to thickness is 200 to 0.5. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 -6 where introducing the filaments (60) into the fractures (50) comprises employing a carrier fluid (38) where a 

 proportion of filaments (60) in the carrier fluid (38) ranges from 0.1 pptg to 200 pptg (0.01 to 24 kg/m 3 ). 14. A fluid (38) for suspending proppants (58) in a hydraulic fracture (50) of a subterranean formation (16), the fluid (38) comprising: a carrier fluid (38); characterized by a plurality of filaments (60), where at least a portion of the filaments (60) comprise a plurality of crosslinkable sites (62) on the filaments (60), where the crosslinkable sites (62) are selected from the group consisting of organo-borate, organo- zirconate, organo-titanate, hydroxyl groups, and combinations thereof; and a plurality of proppants (58). 15. The fluid (38) of claim 14 where the crosslinkable sites (62) on the filaments (60) are chemically bonded to the filaments (60). 16. The fluid (38) of claim 14 where the filament (60) comprises a polymer chain, and where the number of crosslinkable sites (62) along the polymer chain may range from 1 :2 to 1 : 100. molar ratio of crosslinkable sites to polymer chain. 17. The fluid (38) of any one of claims 14-16 where the plurality of filaments (60) is selected from the group consisting of polyvinyl alcohols (PVOH), polylactic acids (PLA), polyglycolic acid (PGA), modified polyethylterephthalate (PET), polyesters, polyamides, polycarbonates, cotton, wool, silk, fiberglass, polyurethanes, aramids, acrylics, nylons, polyethylenes, polypropylenes, celluloses, polylactides, modified polyethylene terephthalates, rayons, and combinations thereof. 18. The fluid (38) of any one of claims 14-16 where the filaments (60) comprise: 

 an average length of from 0.1 inch to 20 inches (0.25 to 51 cm); and an average thickness of from 0.002 inch to 0.2 inch (0.05 mm to 5 mm). 19. The fluid (38) of any one of claims 14-16 where the filaments (60) have a minimum aspect ratio of length to thickness is 200 to 0.5. 20. The fluid (38) of any one of claims 14-16 where a proportion of filaments (60) in the carrier fluid (38) ranges from 0.1 pptg to 50 pptg (0.01 to 6 kg/m 3 ).
